pip-compile.description: Manage the hash-pinned docs lock: read docs/requirements.txt's pip-compile header, bump deps in docs/requirements.in, and regenerate the lock with --generate-hashes.

customManagers.description: Track GREMLINS_VERSION in .github/workflows/mutation.yml — gremlins is `go install`-ed at CI runtime, so neither the gomod nor github-actions manager sees it. Renovate cross-references go-gremlins/gremlins's GitHub releases.

packageRules.description: slsa-github-generator must be called by semver tag, never a commit SHA — the reusable workflow verifies its own ref at runtime and fails when pinned to a digest (documented in the generator's README: builders/generators MUST be referenced by tag). Keep tag bumps, suppress the pinDigest/digest updates the pin preset would otherwise open.
Indirect gomod deps still get bumped — Go's transitive graph is part of the supply chain.
Gremlins (mutation testing) is pinned as GREMLINS_VERSION in the workflow file, not go.mod — keep it on the chore(ci) cadence so its PR title passes the conventional-title check.

docs/requirements.txt is a hash-pinned pip-compile lock (generated from docs/requirements.in). The pip-compile manager updates the .in and regenerates the lock WITH hashes; pip_requirements must NOT touch the lock or it would bump a version without regenerating --hash lines, breaking the --require-hashes install. Keep it disabled for that file.
Docs toolchain (mkdocs-material + transitive deps) bumps land in one PR via the pip-compile lock; chore(deps) keeps the conventional-title check happy.
